description: Introduction to the fundamentals of Computer Aided Engineering. Topics include mathematical and programmable methods for modeling and design of mechanical shapes and assemblies; shape processing for manufacturing, including NC machining and 3D printing; and computer-aided analysis of structural, thermal and other physical properties.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: M E 231, (MATH 320,340,341, or375), (M E 306orE M A 303or concurrent enrollment), (M E 240orE M A 202), and (COMP SCI 200,220,300, 301,310, or placement intoCOMP SCI 300), or member of Engineering Guest Students
